monster.name: The Great Mighty Poo

trait.name: Immortal Mass
entries: The Great Mighty Poo cannot be reduced below 1 hit point.
Any damage dealt to him simply blasts chunks away that immediately reform.

trait.name: The Show Must Go On
entries: The Great Mighty Poo constantly sings.
While singing, he has resistance to all damage and cannot become Flushable.

trait.name: Vulnerable Core
entries: If The Great Mighty Poo is interrupted while singing, his sweetcorn-filled core is exposed until the start of his next turn.
During this time:

items: Toilet Paper attacks can affect him.

trait.name: Interrupting the Performance
entries: The players may expose his core in any of the following ways:

items: Deal 20 damage from a single attack.
Successfully counter one of his songs.
A creature adjacent to him may use an action to heckle him. Make a DC 16 Charisma (Performance, Persuasion, or Intimidation) check. On a success, he becomes enraged and exposes his core.

entries: Reward creative ideas that would interrupt a self-important opera singer.

trait.name: Toilet Paper Hits
entries: While his core is exposed, a creature holding a roll of enchanted toilet paper may use an action to make a ranged attack.

entries.name: Toilet Paper Toss
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 30/60 ft. Hit: The Great Mighty Poo gains 1 Flush Counter.
When he reaches 3 Flush Counters, he becomes Flushable.

trait.name: Flushable
entries: While Flushable, a creature adjacent to the Giant Flush Lever may use an action to activate it.
The Great Mighty Poo must make a DC 18 Strength saving throw.
On a failure, he is instantly defeated.
On a success, one Flush Counter is removed.

trait.name: Filthy
entries: A creature affected by Dung Lob has its speed reduced by 10 feet.
A creature may use an action to clean itself.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Great Mighty Poo makes two Dung Lob attacks.

action.name: Dung Lob
entries: Ranged Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, range 90 ft., one target. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage. The target must succeed on a DC 16 Dexterity saving throw or become Filthy.

action.name: Mighty Slap
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +8 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 17 (3d6 + 6) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Brown Note Aria (Recharge 5-6)
entries: The Great Mighty Poo unleashes a terrible operatic note. Every creature within 60 feet must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 18 (4d8) thunder damage and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage.

legendary: The Great Mighty Poo has 2 legendary actions.

legendary.name: Throw More Poo
entries: The Great Mighty Poo makes one Dung Lob attack.

legendary.name: Dramatic Solo
entries: One creature within 60 feet must succeed on a DC 16 Wisdom saving throw or have disadvantage on its next attack roll.

trait.name: Tiny Target
entries: Opportunity attacks against the Tiny Tiny Dragon are made with disadvantage.

trait.name: Lightning Scurry
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon does not provoke opportunity attacks when it flies out of a creature's reach.

trait.name: Legendary Resistance (2/Day)
entries: If the dragon fails a saving throw, it can choose to succeed instead.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon makes three attacks: one with its Bite and two with its Claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 12 (2d6 + 5) piercing damage plus 9 (2d8) lightning damage.

action.name: Claw
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 9 (1d8 + 5) slashing damage.

action.name: Static Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 11 (1d12 + 5) bludgeoning damage plus 4 (1d8) lightning damage.

action.name: Tiny Frightful Presence
entries: Each creature of the dragon's choice within 30 feet that can see or hear it must succeed on a DC 17 Wisdom saving throw or become frightened until the end of the dragon's next turn. Creatures that succeed are immune to this effect for 24 hours.

action.name: Lightning Breath (Recharge 5-6)
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon exhales lightning in a 30-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in the line must make a DC 18 Dexterity saving throw, taking 45 (10d8) lightning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

bonus.name: Zip
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon flies up to half its flying speed without provoking opportunity attacks.

legendary.name: Detect
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon makes a Wisdom (Perception) check.

legendary.name: Tail Attack
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon makes one Static Tail attack.

legendary.name: Spark Dash (Costs 2 Actions)
entries: The Tiny Tiny Dragon moves up to its flying speed. Each creature whose space it leaves within 5 feet takes 7 (2d6) lightning damage.

trait.name: Swarmform Catastrophe
entries: The horde occupies a 120-foot-long, 60-foot-wide mass and moves as a single entity.
It can pass through openings as small as 1 inch without slowing.
It can climb over itself to scale walls up to 40 feet high.

trait.name: Diminishing Flock
entries: When the horde takes damage, reduce its hit points normally.
Additionally:

items: For every 25 damage dealt in a single turn, its speed is reduced by 5 feet until the end of its next turn.
If it takes 50 or more damage in a single turn, it becomes disorganized and cannot use Chicken Surge on its next turn.

trait.name: Pecking Mass
entries: A creature that starts its turn within 10 feet of the horde or inside it must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw.
On a failed save, the creature takes 16 (3d10) piercing damage and has disadvantage on the next attack roll it makes before the end of its turn.
If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the creature is also blinded until the end of its turn.
On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage.

trait.name: Overwhelming Pressure
entries: When the horde enters a creature's space or passes through it, the creature must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw.
On a failed save, the creature takes 27 (5d10) bludgeoning damage, is pushed 15 feet, and is knocked prone.
If the saving throw fails by 5 or more, the creature is also restrained until the end of its next turn.
On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't moved.

trait.name: Living Avalanche
entries: Structures and barricades are forced to make a DC 18 Strength check when the horde collides with them.
On a failure, they take 30 damage and degrade (DM's discretion).

trait.name: Momentum Swarm
entries: The horde ignores nonmagical difficult terrain and slowing effects.
Magical effects reduce its speed by half for 1 round before it adapts.

trait.name: Rooster Apex (240 HP or Fewer)
entries: When the horde is reduced to 240 hit points or fewer, a massive rooster erupts from the swarm and takes command.
While the Rooster Apex lives:

items: The horde's speed increases by 10 feet.
Pecking Mass damage increases to 27 (5d10) piercing damage.

entries: The Rooster Apex acts on initiative count 20 (losing initiative ties).

trait.name: Legendary Movement
entries: At initiative count 20, the horde moves up to 20 feet.
If it moves at least 20 feet, each creature in its path must succeed on a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw or be knocked prone.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The horde makes two Pecking Mass attacks.

action.name: Pecking Mass
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +12 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 22 (4d10) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 17 Strength saving throw or be pulled into the horde's space.

action.name: Chicken Surge (Recharge 5-6)
entries: The horde erupts forward in a 60-foot push. Each creature in its path must make a DC 17 Strength saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes 44 (8d10) bludgeoning damage, is pushed 20 feet, and is knocked prone. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage and is pushed 10 feet.

action.name: Featherstorm Eruption (Recharge 6)
entries: The horde flaps and panics violently. Each creature within 30 feet must make a DC 16 Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature is blinded until the end of its next turn and has disadvantage on Constitution saving throws made to maintain concentration. On a successful save, the creature suffers no additional effect.
